issuesFound.title: missing page language
issuesFound.explanation: The <html> element is missing a lang attribute, which prevents screen readers from using the correct pronunciation and voice profile for the content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2: <html lang="en">

issuesFound.title: missing page title
issuesFound.explanation: The <title> element is empty, which means the page has no accessible name in the browser tab or window list, making it difficult for users to identify the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7: <title>Luminary Studio — Accessible Design & Productivity Tools</title>

issuesFound.title: toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button lacks an aria-expanded attribute, so screen reader users are not informed whether the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 41: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-expanded="false" aria-label="Toggle navigation menu">

issuesFound.fix: Line 101: <nav aria-label="Account">

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on html
issuesFound.explanation: The global focus-visible style is set to outline: none. While a border-radius is defined, no visible replacement (like box-shadow or a different outline) is provided, leaving keyboard users without a visual cue.
issuesFound.fix: Line 185: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The focus-visible state for all buttons explicitly removes the outline. Although transitions are defined for other properties, no visible focus indicator is rendered when navigating via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 465: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link in the header removes the focus outline. This prevents keyboard users from identifying when the site's primary home link is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 545: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle removes the focus outline. Keyboard users on mobile-width viewports will not be able to see their focus position on the menu button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 578: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links remove the focus outline. While a border-radius is set, there is no visible change in border, background, or outline to indicate focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 641: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the default outline on focus. While it adds a box-shadow, the shadow uses a low-opacity blue (0.2) which may not be sufficiently visible as a focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 674: #search-input:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input explicitly removes the outline-offset and outline for focus-visible, negating the browser's default accessibility features.
issuesFound.fix: Line 681: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submission button removes the focus outline, leaving keyboard users unable to track focus within the search form.
issuesFound.fix: Line 704: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Links within the account navigation remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative style.
issuesFound.fix: Line 731: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The "Learn more" links in feature cards remove the focus outline, making it difficult for keyboard users to navigate the features grid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 914: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Product filter buttons remove the focus outline, which blocks keyboard users from knowing which filter they are currently interacting with.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1065: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing billing toggle buttons remove the focus outline, failing to provide a visible focus state for keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1231: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table region is focusable (tabindex="0") but removes the focus outline, meaning keyboard users won't know they have focused the region to scroll it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1365: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove the focus outline, preventing keyboard users from identifying which question they are about to expand.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1504: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove the default outline. While a box-shadow is added, it uses a low-opacity (0.2) color that may not be visible enough for all users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1548: .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs explicitly remove the focus-visible outline, which is a direct failure of focus visibility requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1549: .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs remove the focus outline, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which checkbox is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1562: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class sets position: static, width: auto, and height: auto. This fails to visually hide the content, and the lack of absolute positioning/clipping means it will interfere with the visual layout.
issuesFound.fix: Line 214: position: absolute; Line 215: width: 1px; Line 216: height: 1px; Line 219: overflow: hidden; Line 220: clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0);

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition to slide into view. There is no override in the prefers-reduced-motion block to disable this movement for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 278: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: translateY(0); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class includes a transform transition. This causes movement on hover/active states that is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 278: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The primary hero CTA includes a transform transition for its hover effect. This movement is not suppressed in the reduced motion media query.
issuesFound.fix: Line 278: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card a::after
issuesFound.explanation: The arrow in the feature card link uses a transform transition to move horizontally. This animation lacks a reduced motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 908: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card a::after { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .faq-item dt button::after
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion icon uses a transform transition to rotate. While a media query exists on line 1508, it only sets transition: none, which may leave the icon in an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1508: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .faq-item dt button::after { transition: none; transform: rotate(90deg); } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing to -0.2em. Negative word spacing crowds text together, making it significantly harder for users with dyslexia or low vision to distinguish individual words.
issuesFound.fix: Line 145: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing to -0.05em. Tightening the space between characters reduces legibility, especially at smaller font sizes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 146: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The hero heading uses -0.05em letter-spacing. While used for aesthetic "tight" headings, it reduces character definition for users with visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 778: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .stat-value
issuesFound.explanation: Large statistical numbers use -0.05em letter-spacing. This can cause numbers to bleed together, making data points harder to read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1154: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .price-amount
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing amounts use -0.05em letter-spacing. This impairs the readability of critical financial information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1295: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global 'a' selector removes text-decoration. In blocks of body text (like paragraphs), links will only be identifiable by their color, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users with color blindness.
issuesFound.fix: Line 161: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has an explicit height of 28px. This is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 24px for target size and significantly below the 44px recommended for comfortable touch interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 661: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] height/width 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is 28px by 28px. This is a small target for touch users and users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 690: width: 44px; Line 691: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 20px. This is below the absolute WCAG 2.2 minimum of 24px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 518: min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon height/width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Icon buttons are set to 20px by 20px, which is too small for reliable activation by many users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 531: min-width: 44px; Line 532: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #nav-menu > li > a min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links have a min-height of 28px. While above the 24px minimum, they are close together and lack sufficient vertical target area for AA 2.2 recommendations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 614: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: [id$="-submenu"] li a min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links have a min-height of 28px, creating a dense list of small targets that are difficult to trigger accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 637: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.3:1
issuesFound.explanation: The secondary text color (--colour-text-secondary) and focus ring color are set to #aaaaaa on a white background. This ratio of 2.3:1 is well below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 78: --colour-text-secondary: #707070; Line 88: --colour-focus-ring: #707070;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #ee9999 / background #ffffff ratio 2.4:1
issuesFound.explanation: The error color (--colour-error) used for required markers and danger buttons is #ee9999. On a white background, this fails the 4.5:1 contrast requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 69: --colour-error: #d32f2f;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #555555 / background #1e293b ratio 3.1:1
issuesFound.explanation: The dark background (--colour-bg-dark / --colour-neutral-800) paired with primary text (--colour-neutral-900 / #555555) in the footer or dark sections fails the 4.5:1 ratio.
issuesFound.fix: Line 61: --colour-neutral-900: #ffffff; (for use on dark backgrounds)

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation toggle the `is-open` class and manage scroll locking, but they do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the navigation menu has been deployed or retracted.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1545: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line 1558: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function switches the product grid between 'grid' and 'list' modes and updates a CSS active class, but it uses `aria-pressed` only as a string literal in a `setAttr` call without ensuring the attribute is toggled correctly on the actual clicked element during the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2415: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true');` Line 2424: `setAttr(b, ARIA.PRESSED, String(b === btn));`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the product filter is applied, the `_updateCountBadge` function updates the text content of the badge, but there is no immediate write to an ARIA live region to announce the new number of results to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2345: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.searchResultsFound(count, _productFilterState.searchQuery));`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module updates the error message text and toggles the `is-invalid` CSS class, but it fails to set the `aria-invalid="true"` attribute on the input field itself. Screen readers will not perceive the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3215: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is validated as successful, the `_renderResult` function removes the error message and CSS classes but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute. A field previously marked invalid will continue to be announced as invalid by some screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3210: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the trigger and drawer elements but does not explicitly set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the trigger. If the HTML does not include this attribute, the initial state is ambiguous to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1605: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation toggle the `is-open` class and body scroll lock but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the navigation menu has been deployed or retracted.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1545: `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line 1561: `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function switches the product grid between 'grid' and 'list' modes and updates a CSS class, but it uses `aria-pressed` as a static string rather than updating the state dynamically on the buttons. This prevents assistive technology from tracking which view mode is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2325: `setAttr(b, ARIA.PRESSED, mode === b.getAttribute('data-view-mode'));`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the `_updateCountBadge` function updates the text content of the count element, but there is no call to a live region to announce the new result count. Users of screen readers will not know how many products remain after a filter is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2245: `_countBadge.textContent = String(count); liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(count));`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module applies CSS classes and shows/hides error messages but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not programmatically perceive the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3115: `else { field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field passes validation, the `_renderResult` function removes error classes but does not reset the `aria-invalid` attribute to 'false'. The field may continue to be announced as invalid even after the user has corrected the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3109: `if (result.valid) { field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'false'); srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the trigger and drawer elements but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state. If the menu is closed by default, the trigger button lacks the necessary state for screen readers to understand its purpose until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1635: `_initialised = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element

issuesFound.fix: Line 250: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons have outline: none on focus without a visible replacement, hiding focus indicators for keyboard users. This prevents users from navigating buttons effectively.
issuesFound.fix: Line 350: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. This can make buttons difficult to tap for users with motor impairments or on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 370: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-4); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Small interactive targets are hard to activate accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transform and transition properties that cause motion on hover, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for this selector is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience animation, which can cause discomfort.
issuesFound.fix: Line 350: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em, which reduces spacing between words and letters. This can decrease readability, especially for users with dyslexia or visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 110: body { word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on a background likely var(--colour-bg-page) (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, below the 4.5:1 required for normal text. Users with low vision may struggle to read the text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 290: .section-header p { color: var(--colour-neutral-600); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Links are distinguished only by color changes, which may not be perceivable by users with colour vision deficiencies or in low-contrast situations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 130: a { text-decoration: underline; } or add consistent non-color indicators such as underline on hover.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. This prevents keyboard users from seeing which element has focus, blocking access to interactive content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 298: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input's focus-visible state removes outline without a replacement. Keyboard users lose visual feedback when focusing the search field, affecting form accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 617: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm selector sets min-height to 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments or on touch devices may struggle to activate this button accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 497: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-4); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon rule defines min-width and min-height as 20px, below the 24px minimum. Small touch targets are difficult to tap, especially for users with limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 504: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: .nav-toggle has min-height and min-width of 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target. While padding may increase the area, the explicit size is insufficient for reliable interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 544: .nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; padding: var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button
issuesFound.explanation: These navigation links and buttons have a min-height of 28px, below the 44px recommendation. This can cause touch inaccuracies for mobile users or those with motor challenges.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-4); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links have min-height set to 28px, which is below the 44px touch target guideline. Users may mis-tap these items, degrading the experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 589: [id$="-submenu"] li a { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-4); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input height is 28px, below the 44px recommended minimum. Small interactive elements are hard to target precisely with touch or mouse.
issuesFound.fix: Line 609: #search-input { height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-4); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has width and height of 28px, below the 24px minimum. This small target size can lead to activation errors for users with motor disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 625: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons have min-height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommendation. Inadequate touch targets impede filtering interactions for touchscreen users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 832: .filter-tabs button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-5); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs are set to 20px width and height, below the 24px minimum. Users with dexterity issues may find it difficult to check or uncheck these boxes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1102: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and visible overflow, making content visible on screen instead of hidden. Screen reader users may hear duplicated content, while visual users see hidden text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 341: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transform transition that creates spatial movement, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 724 is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort from unnecessary animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 724: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing to -0.2em, which compresses text and can reduce readability, especially for users with dyslexia or visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 239: body { word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: All links have text-decoration: none, removing underlines. Since colour is the only visual differentiator, users with colour blindness or low vision may not distinguish links from regular text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 227: a { text-decoration: underline; color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation menu toggle button does not update its aria-expanded attribute when the menu is opened or closed. Screen reader users and keyboard users cannot perceive the current state of the menu, violating WCAG 2.1.1 and 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2525: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 2545: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) do not set aria-pressed to reflect the active state. Screen reader users cannot perceive which option is selected, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4325: In `_syncToggleState`, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the updated result count is not announced to screen readers via a live region. Users with visual impairments may not know the content has changed, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3925: In `applyFilters`, after `emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : 'Filter applied');`.

issuesFound.title: billing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing period (monthly/annual) does not announce the change to screen readers via a live region. Users with visual impairments may not know the pricing display has updated, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4345: In `setPeriod`, after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Inline validation errors do not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid input fields. Screen reader users may not be alerted to field errors, violating WCAG 3.3.1 and 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5225: In `_renderResult`, inside the else block (invalid case), add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`. In the valid case, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have aria-expanded initialised to "false" when the page loads with the menu closed. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2515: In the `init` function of mobileNav, after `_initialised = true;`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded initialised to "false" when the page loads with panels hidden. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2715: In the `register` function of dropdownMenu, after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open, close, and toggle functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button. Screen-reader users will not be informed whether the navigation menu is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu change the panel visibility but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern for screen-reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2150: function _openInstance(inst) { ... ; setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); }
        Line 2165: function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { ... ; setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); }

issuesFound.title: Site search suggestion panel open/close does not update aria-expanded on input
issuesFound.explanation: The _open and _close functions in siteSearch control the visibility of the suggestion listbox but do not set aria-expanded on the search input (which has role="combobox" and aria-haspopup="listbox"). Screen-reader users won't know if the suggestions are expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3078: function _open() { ... ; setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); }
        Line 3087: function _close() { ... ; setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); }

issuesFound.title: View mode toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed when toggled
issuesFound.explanation: The _onViewToggle function changes the view mode (grid/list) but does not set aria-pressed on the view toggle buttons (data-view-mode). Screen-reader users cannot tell which view is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2629: function _onViewToggle(e) { ... ; qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => { setAttr(b, ARIA.PRESSED, b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === mode); }); }

issuesFound.title: Product filter count update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function updates the visible product count but does not announce this change via an ARIA live region. Screen-reader users are not informed when the number of visible products changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2545: function applyFilters() { ... ; liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`); }

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod function changes the billing period and updates prices but does not announce the change via an ARIA live region. Screen-reader users are not informed when the billing period switches.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3834: function setPeriod(period, animate) { ... ; liveRegion.announce(`Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`); }

issuesFound.title: FAQ item open/close not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The open and close functions in faqAccordion expand/collapse FAQ answers but do not announce the state change via an ARIA live region. Screen-reader users are not informed when an answer becomes visible or hidden.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors do not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult function in inlineValidator marks invalid fields with a CSS class but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state of the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4245: function _renderResult(field, result, errorEl) { ... ; setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, !result.valid); }

issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation menu is closed on page load, but the trigger button (hamburger) does not have aria-expanded="false" set initially. Screen-reader users will not know the initial collapsed state.

issuesFound.explanation: Links that fill an entire feature card lose their outline on :focus-visible and only set outline‑offset, providing no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ toggle buttons remove the outline on :focus-visible and only set a negative outline‑offset, leaving no visible focus cue.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the outline on :focus-visible and only sets outline‑offset, providing no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing period toggle buttons remove the outline on :focus-visible and only set outline‑offset, leaving no visible focus cue.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 44 × 44 px, making it difficult for finger navigation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon‑only button sets min‑width and min‑height to 20 px, below the required 44 px target area, hindering touch interaction.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img:hover

issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button in the hero section animates transform on hover without a reduced‑motion fallback, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for motion‑sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: ‑0.05em, which compresses word gaps and reduces legibility, especially for users with dyslexia or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on a
issuesFound.explanation: Global a selector removes underlines (text‑decoration: none) and relies solely on colour to differentiate links, which may not be sufficient for users with low colour perception.

issuesFound.explanation: The global “:focus-visible” selector removes the outline (outline: none) and does not provide any alternative visible focus style. Keyboard‑only users lose the ability to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions in **mobileNav** change the visual state of the navigation drawer but never set `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) is used as a two‑state toggle, yet its `aria‑pressed` state is never changed, leaving AT users without feedback.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks initial aria‑expanded and aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation:
On page load the trigger button has no `aria-expanded="false"` or `aria-pressed="false"` attributes, so the initial state is ambiguous to assistive tech.  
Fix (in `mobileNav.init()` after `_trigger` is obtained):  
```javascript
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed',  'false');
```

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance()` adds/removes the panel but never toggles `aria‑expanded` on the button that controls it, breaking the disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation:
When a dropdown is registered, the button receives `aria-haspopup` but no `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, so its collapsed state is not conveyed.  
Fix (inside `register()` after creating `inst`):  
```javascript
if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) {
  setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');   // ← add
}
```

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: `open(item)` and `close(item)` change the panel visibility but never set `aria‑expanded` on the question button, leaving the accordion’s state invisible to AT.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation:
During parsing, the trigger button gets `aria-controls` but no `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, so its default collapsed state is not announced.  
Fix (in `faqRegistry.parse()` after `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);`):  
```javascript
setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');   // ← add
```

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation:
The element with `data-billing-toggle` acts as a binary switch (monthly ↔ annual) but its `aria‑pressed` state is never changed, so screen‑reader users cannot tell the current period.  
Fix (inside `_syncToggleState()` after class toggling):  
```javascript
if (_toggleEl) {
  setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', isYear ? 'true' : 'false'); // ← add
}
```

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button missing initial aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation:
On page load the toggle has no `aria-pressed` attribute, making its default state unclear.  
Fix (in `pricing.init()` after `_toggleEl` is obtained):  
```javascript
setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', 'false');   // default is “monthly”
```

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on error fields
Product‑filter result count not announced via live region
Billing‑period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation:
When the user switches between monthly, quarterly, or annual billing, the UI updates prices but no live‑region announcement is made, so AT users do not know the period has changed.  
Fix (add at the end of `setPeriod` after UI updates):  
```javascript
liveRegion.announce(`Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label.toLowerCase()}`);
```

fpTitles: FAQ accordion trigger does not update aria‑expanded
Billing‑period toggle button missing initial aria‑pressed

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In the dropdown menu controller, the `_onTriggerClick` handler (lines 1150‑1155) toggles the dropdown panel’s visibility but never updates `aria‑expanded` on the button that controls it. Assistive technologies therefore receive no state information.
issuesFound.fix: After opening or closing a dropdown, call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', isOpen ? 'true' : 'false');` where `trigger` is the button element.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing aria‑expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ parser (`faqRegistry.parse`) (lines 2100‑2105) determines the initial open state but does **not** set `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button when the item is closed. Consequently, on page load closed items have no explicit ARIA state.
issuesFound.fix: After creating `trigger`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', isInitiallyOpen ? 'true' : 'false');` to ensure the attribute is always present.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: In the pricing module (`pricing._syncToggleState`) (lines 2500‑2505) the visual “active” state of period buttons is reflected only by CSS classes. No `aria‑pressed` attribute is set, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which billing period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Inside the loop that iterates over `_periodBtns`, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', btnPeriod === period ? 'true' : 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑toggle switch does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off billing toggle (`pricing._syncToggleState`) (lines 2510‑2515) toggles a CSS class on the track element but never updates `aria‑pressed` on the toggle control (`SELECTORS.toggle`). Users of assistive technology receive no state feedback.
issuesFound.fix: After determining `isYear`, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', isYear ? 'true' : 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator.validate` function (lines 3000‑3005) calls `srUtils.markInvalid`, which only adds `aria‑describedby` but never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on the offending field. Assistive technologies therefore cannot announce the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `validate`, after a failed validation, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` before calling `srUtils.markInvalid`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not clear aria‑invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, `inlineValidator.validate` calls `srUtils.markValid`, which removes `aria‑describedby` but does not remove or set `aria‑invalid="false"`. The stale `aria‑invalid` attribute can mislead AT.
issuesFound.fix: In the success branch of `validate`, add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` (or set it to `"false"`).

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter dynamic UI changes are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter.applyFilters` function (lines 2600‑2605) updates the result count badge, empty‑state message, and pagination, but no ARIA live region is updated to announce these changes. Users of screen readers receive no feedback about filter results.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the UI, call `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches===1?'':'s'} found`);` (or a similar message) and ensure a hidden live region exists in the markup.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period changes are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing.setPeriod` method (lines 2500‑2505) switches prices and updates UI, but does not write to any ARIA live region. Users are not informed when the billing period changes (e.g., “Annual billing selected”).
issuesFound.fix: After the UI update, add `liveRegion.announce( period === 'year' ? 'Annual billing selected' : period === 'quarter' ? 'Quarterly billing selected' : 'Monthly billing selected' );`. Ensure a live region element exists (or reuse the global one).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions change the drawer’s visibility but never set `aria‑expanded` on the hamburger `_trigger` button. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the navigation is open.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` the panel is shown/hidden, but the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` state is never updated, leaving assistive technologies out of sync with the UI.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not update aria‑expanded on its triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` and `faqAccordion.close()` functions expand/collapse the answer panel but never set `aria‑expanded` on the question button, breaking the required ARIA state for accordions.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count is not announced to AT (dynamic content)
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count is updated in the badge (`_updateCountBadge`) but no live‑region is used, so screen‑reader users are not informed of the new result count.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce the savings message (dynamic content)
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched, the global savings message (`_savingsMsg`) is shown/hidden, but no ARIA live region is used, so the change is invisible to screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` calls `srUtils.markInvalid()` which adds an error class and describedby but never sets the required `aria‑invalid="true"` attribute, violating WCAG 2.1 (ARIA‑invalid).

issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` utility class incorrectly uses `position: static` and `clip: auto`, which does not hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. The current implementation fails to hide content from sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img` and `.hero-image img:hover` rules include transform animations but lack a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or slow these animations. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort or distraction.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in `mobileNav` toggle the `is-open` class on the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). This prevents screen readers from announcing the open/closed state of the navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10523: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
    Line 10530: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-pressed on toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick` function in `pricing` toggles the billing period between "month" and "year" but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle button (`_toggleEl`). This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen readers cannot announce the pressed state of the toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12120: `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, String(next === 'year'));`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in `mobileNav` change the state of the navigation menu but do not announce the change to screen readers via a live region. Users who rely on screen readers are not informed of the menu's open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10523: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
    Line 10530: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in `inlineValidator` updates the visual error state but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the invalid field. This violates WCAG 3.3.1, as screen readers cannot announce the invalid state of the field.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13250: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`
    Line 13260: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `mobileNav` does not initialise the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`) to reflect the closed state of the navigation menu on page load. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as the initial state is not programmatically determinable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10560: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module uses a click handler (`_onTriggerClick`) on the navigation toggle button (`_trigger`) but does not provide a corresponding keyboard handler for `Enter` and `Space` keys. This violates WCAG 2.1.1, as keyboard users cannot activate the navigation menu.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing role and keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing` module uses a click handler (`_onToggleClick`) on the billing toggle button (`_toggleEl`) but does not set a `role` attribute (e.g., `role="switch"` or `role="button"`) if the element is not a native `<button>`. Additionally, it lacks keyboard support for `Enter` and `Space` keys. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 and 2.1.1.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content injected without adjacent ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in `inlineValidator` injects error messages into the DOM using `errorEl.textContent` but does not place these messages inside an ARIA live region. Screen readers do not announce these dynamic error messages, violating WCAG 4.1.3.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in `inlineValidator` does not reset the `aria-invalid` attribute to `false` when a field becomes valid. This violates WCAG 3.3.1, as screen readers continue to announce the field as invalid even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 13250: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-required not set on required form fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `bind` function in `inlineValidator` does not set the `aria-required` attribute on form fields that have the `required` validation rule. This violates WCAG 3.3.2, as screen readers cannot announce that the field is required.

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute on personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields for `full_name` (line 13850), `email` (line 13860), and `message` (line 13870) do not have `autocomplete` attributes. This violates WCAG 1.3.5, as browsers cannot autofill these fields, making form completion harder for users with cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13850: `<input name="full_name" autocomplete="name">`
    Line 13860: `<input name="email" autocomplete="email">`
    Line 13870: `<textarea name="message" autocomplete="off">` (Note: `off` is appropriate for message fields to prevent autofill of unrelated data.)

issuesFound.title: aria-controls reference to non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The `register` function in `dropdownMenu` sets `aria-controls` on the trigger button to reference the panel's ID, but it does not ensure the panel has an ID if it is missing. This violates WCAG 1.3.1, as the `aria-controls` reference is broken.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10730: Replace the panel ID assignment with:
    ```
    const panelId = srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel');
    ```

issuesFound.title: aria-labelledby reference to non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The `parse` function in `faqRegistry` sets `aria-labelledby` on the panel to reference the trigger's ID, but it does not ensure the trigger has an ID if it is missing. This violates WCAG 1.3.1, as the `aria-labelledby` reference is broken.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 11420: Replace the trigger ID assignment with:
    ```
    const triggerId = srUtils.ensureId(trigger, 'faq-trigger');
    ```

issuesFound.title: aria-describedby reference to non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The `_getOrCreateErrorEl` function in `inlineValidator` sets `aria-describedby` on the field to reference the error element's ID, but it does not ensure the error element has an ID if it is missing. This violates WCAG 1.3.1, as the `aria-describedby` reference is broken.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13230: Replace the error element creation with:
    ```
    const existingId = `${srUtils.ensureId(field, 'field')}-error`;
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing role on non-semantic interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `planComparisonHighlighter` does not set a `role` attribute (e.g., `role="button"`) on the column header elements (`[data-plan-head]`) that are clickable. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen readers cannot identify these elements as interactive.

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for non-native interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `planComparisonHighlighter` adds a click handler to column headers (`[data-plan-head] a, [data-plan-head] button`) but does not provide a keyboard handler for `Enter` and `Space` keys. This violates WCAG 2.1.1, as keyboard users cannot activate the column highlighting.

issuesFound.title: Missing focus management on modal open
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in `mobileNav` does not move focus to the first focusable element inside the navigation drawer when opened. This violates WCAG 2.4.3, as keyboard users are not directed to the new content.

issuesFound.title: Missing focus return on modal close
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function in `mobileNav` does not return focus to the trigger button (`_trigger`) when the navigation drawer is closed. This violates WCAG 2.4.3, as keyboard users lose their place in the page.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10530: Add the following before closing the drawer:
    ```
    if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing focus trap on modal dialog
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in `mobileNav` does not implement a focus trap inside the navigation drawer. This violates WCAG 2.1.2, as keyboard users can tab out of the drawer and lose context.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-modal on modal dialog
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in `mobileNav` does not set the `aria-modal="true"` attribute on the navigation drawer (`_drawer`). This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen readers cannot identify the drawer as a modal dialog.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10523: Add the following after the drawer is opened:
    ```
    setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.MODAL, 'true');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-modal cleanup on modal close
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function in `mobileNav` does not remove the `aria-modal` attribute from the navigation drawer (`_drawer`) when closed. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as the attribute remains incorrectly set.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10530: Add the following before closing the drawer:
    ```
    removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.MODAL);
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing role on modal dialog
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in `mobileNav` does not set a `role="dialog"` attribute on the navigation drawer (`_drawer`). This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen readers cannot identify the drawer as a dialog.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10523: Add the following after the drawer is opened:
    ```
    setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.ROLE, 'dialog');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing role cleanup on modal close
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function in `mobileNav` does not remove the `role="dialog"` attribute from the navigation drawer (`_drawer`) when closed. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as the role remains incorrectly set.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10530: Add the following before closing the drawer:
    ```
    removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.ROLE);
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for modal dialog
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in `mobileNav` does not set an `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` attribute on the navigation drawer (`_drawer`). This violates WCAG 2.5.3, as screen readers cannot announce the purpose of the dialog.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10523: Add the following after the drawer is opened:
    ```
    setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.LABEL, STRINGS.labels.openMenu || 'Navigation menu');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for dropdown menu
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function in `dropdownMenu` does not set an `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` attribute on the dropdown panel (`inst.panel`). This violates WCAG 2.5.3, as screen readers cannot announce the purpose of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10710: Add the following after the panel is opened:
    ```
    setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.LABEL, STRINGS.labels.dropdownMenu || 'Dropdown menu');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for FAQ answer panel
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in `faqAccordion` does not ensure the FAQ answer panel (`item.panel`) has an `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` attribute. This violates WCAG 2.5.3, as screen readers cannot announce the purpose of the panel.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 11450: Add the following in the `parse` function of `faqRegistry`:
    ```
    setAttr(panel, ARIA.LABELLEDBY, triggerId);
    ```

This prevents screen readers from announcing the open/close state of the navigation menu, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 11505: `if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return;`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `mobileNav` module does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button (`_trigger`) when the navigation drawer is closed on page load. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 by not providing an initial state for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 11560: `if (!_trigger || !_drawer) { debug('mobileNav.init: trigger or drawer not found — skipping'); return; }`
    Add after this line: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in the `mobileNav` module changes the visible state of the navigation drawer but does not announce this change to screen readers via a live region. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) by not providing feedback about the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 11515: `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`
    Add after this line: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` function in the `inlineValidator` module adds error classes and inserts error messages but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 by not exposing the invalid state to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` function in the `inlineValidator` module does not reset `aria-invalid` to `false` when a field becomes valid. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 by leaving the field marked as invalid even after correction.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module uses a click handler (`_onTriggerClick`) on the navigation toggle button but does not provide a matching keyboard handler for `Enter` or `Space` keys. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) by preventing keyboard users from activating the navigation menu.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content injected without adjacent ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `suggestionListRenderer.render` function injects dynamic content into the DOM using `innerHTML` but does not update an adjacent ARIA live region to announce the new suggestions to screen readers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_validateCurrentStep` function in the `multiStepWizard` module validates form fields but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid fields. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 by not exposing the invalid state to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers

    ```
    liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');
    ```

This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the initial state is not programmatically determinable.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module updates the visual error state of form fields but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute to `true` when a field is invalid. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification) as screen readers cannot programmatically determine that a field contains an error.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module does not remove the `aria-invalid` attribute when a field becomes valid. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the invalid state persists even after correction.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module's trigger button (`.nav-toggle`) has a click event listener but lacks keyboard event handlers for `Enter` and `Space` keys. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) as keyboard users cannot activate the navigation menu.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing role and tabindex
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle button (`[data-billing-toggle]`) in the `pricing` module is not assigned a `role="switch"` or `role="button"` and lacks a `tabindex="0"` if it is not a native `<button>` element. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as the element is not programmatically identifiable as interactive.

issuesFound.title: Focus not managed on modal open/close
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module opens and closes a navigation drawer but does not manage focus programmatically. On open, focus should move to the first focusable element inside the drawer. On close, focus should return to the trigger button. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order).

issuesFound.title: Focus not returned on modal close
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function in the `dropdownMenu` module closes a dropdown panel but does not return focus to the trigger button. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) as keyboard users lose their place in the tab order.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10510: Replace `function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) {` with:
    ```
    function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus = true) {
    ```
    Add after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`
    ```
    if (returnFocus) inst.trigger.focus();
    ```

---

issuesFound.title: setTimeout/setInterval changing visible content without pause/stop control
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel` module uses `setTimeout` to auto-advance slides (`_scheduleNext` function) but does not provide a user-controllable pause/stop mechanism. This violates WCAG 2.2.2 (Pause, Stop, Hide) as users cannot pause auto-rotating content.

issuesFound.title: Content injected via innerHTML without adjacent ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `suggestionListRenderer.render` function injects HTML into the suggestion list using `innerHTML` but does not update an adjacent ARIA live region to announce the new suggestions to screen readers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) as dynamic search results are not announced.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing role and keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField.bind` function enhances a `<div>` into a drag-and-drop upload zone but does not assign a `role="button"` or ensure keyboard operability via `Enter`/`Space` keys. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) and 2.1.1 (Keyboard).

issuesFound.title: Broken aria-controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function in the `dropdownMenu` module assumes that the panel element has an `id` attribute. If the panel lacks an `id`, the `aria-controls` reference on the trigger button becomes invalid, violating WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships).

issuesFound.title: Broken aria-labelledby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in the `faqAccordion` module assumes that the FAQ panel has an `aria-labelledby` attribute pointing to the trigger button. If the trigger button lacks an `id`, the reference becomes invalid, violating WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships).

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button (`.nav-toggle`) lacks an accessible name. While it may have visible text, screen readers require an explicit `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` to announce its purpose. This violates WCAG 2.5.3 (Label in Name) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute on personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields (`#contact-first-name`, `#contact-last-name`, `#contact-email`, `#contact-company`, `#contact-team-size`) lack `autocomplete` attributes. This violates WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose) as browsers and assistive technologies cannot programmatically determine the purpose of these fields.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13500: `#contact-first-name`: `autocomplete="given-name"`
    Line 13510: `#contact-last-name`: `autocomplete="family-name"`
    Line 13520: `#contact-email`: `autocomplete="email"`
    Line 13530: `#contact-company`: `autocomplete="organization"`
    Line 13540: `#contact-team-size`: `autocomplete="off"` (not personal data)

---

issuesFound.title: Missing fieldset and legend for grouped inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (`#billing-monthly`, `#billing-annual`) are visually grouped but lack a `<fieldset>` and `<legend>` to programmatically associate them. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as screen readers cannot announce the group's purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12030: Wrap the toggle buttons in:
    ```
    <fieldset>
      <legend>Billing period</legend>
      <!-- toggle buttons here -->
    </fieldset>
    ```

---

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-live region for dynamic form status
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter and contact forms update their status messages (success/error) visually but do not use an `aria-live` region to announce these changes to screen readers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) as dynamic feedback is not announced.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-atomic on live region
issuesFound.explanation: The status message live regions in the newsletter and contact forms lack `aria-atomic="true"`, which may cause screen readers to announce only the changed portion of the message rather than the entire message. This violates best practices for live regions.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13450: Add `'aria-atomic': 'true'` to the status element attributes.
    Line 13500: Add `'aria-atomic': 'true'` to the status element attributes.

---

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-relevant on live region
issuesFound.explanation: The status message live regions in the newsletter and contact forms lack `aria-relevant="text"`, which may cause screen readers to announce irrelevant changes (e.g., attribute changes). This violates best practices for live regions.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13450: Add `'aria-relevant': 'text'` to the status element attributes.
    Line 13500: Add `'aria-relevant': 'text'` to the status element attributes.

---

issuesFound.title: Missing role="alert" for error messages
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module inserts error messages with `role="alert"` but does not ensure that the error element is empty before reusing it. This may cause screen readers to miss new error messages if the previous message was not cleared. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).

issuesFound.title: Missing role="alert" for error summary
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderErrorSummary` function in the `contactForm` module creates an error summary with `role="alert"` but does not ensure that the summary is empty before reusing it. This may cause screen readers to miss new error summaries. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on iframe
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.labelIframes` function ensures that `<iframe>` elements have a `title` attribute, but it does not ensure that the `title` is descriptive. A generic title like "Content frame 1" does not meet WCAG 2.5.3 (Label in Name) as it does not convey the iframe's purpose.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on progressbar
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonials carousel progress dots (`[role="tab"]`) lack `aria-label` attributes to describe their purpose. This violates WCAG 2.5.3 (Label in Name) as screen readers cannot announce the purpose of each dot.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 11800: Replace the dot creation block with:
    ```
    const dot = createElement('button', {
      type: 'button',
      role: 'tab',
      'aria-selected': String(i === _current),
      'aria-label': `Go to testimonial ${i + 1}`,
      'aria-controls': slide.el.id || '',
      tabindex: i === _current ? '0' : '-1',
    });
    ```

---

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on scroll-to-top button
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button (`#scroll-top-btn`) lacks an `aria-label` attribute. While it may have visible text, screen readers require an explicit label to announce its purpose. This violates WCAG 2.5.3 (Label in Name) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on dismiss button
issuesFound.explanation: The toast notification dismiss button (`.toast__dismiss`) lacks an `aria-label` attribute. While it may have visible text ("×"), screen readers require an explicit label to announce its purpose. This violates WCAG 2.5.3 (Label in Name) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on file upload zone
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload zone created by `fileUploadField.bind` lacks an `aria-label` attribute. While it may have a `data-upload-label` attribute, screen readers require an explicit `aria-label` to announce its purpose. This violates WCAG 2.5.3 (Label in Name) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12800: Replace the `aria-label` assignment with:
    ```
    setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-label',
      zoneEl.getAttribute('data-upload-label') ||
      (STRINGS.labels.uploadFiles || 'Upload files – click or drag files here')
    );
    ```

---

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input (`#search-input`) lacks an `aria-label` attribute. While it may have a visible label, screen readers require an explicit `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` to announce its purpose. This violates WCAG 2.5.3 (Label in Name) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on clear search button
issuesFound.explanation: The clear search button (`.search-clear`) lacks an `aria-label` attribute. While it may have visible text, screen readers require an explicit label to announce its purpose. This violates WCAG 2.5.3 (Label in Name) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix:
(fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script)
    Add `aria-label="Clear search"` to the clear search button in the HTML or via JavaScript during initialisation.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on cookie consent buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent banner buttons ("Accept all", "Essential only") lack `aria-label` attributes. While they have visible text, screen readers may not announce their purpose clearly in all contexts. This violates WCAG 2.5.3 (Label in Name) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component in the MobileNav close button uses a "✕" icon without an aria-label prop. issuesFound.title: Toggle component trigger missing aria-expanded prop
issuesFound.explanation: The button controlling the mobile navigation dropdown in MobileNav does not receive an aria-expanded prop to indicate the expanded/collapsed state of the submenu. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot announce the state of the disclosure.
issuesFound.fix: Line 968: `<button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>`

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component in the SearchInput form does not receive an aria-invalid prop when the input is in an invalid state. While the component supports an invalid prop, it is not passed through in this usage, preventing screen readers from announcing validation errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1305: `<TextInput id={id} type="search" value={value} onChange={(ev) => onChange(ev.target.value)} placeholder={placeholder} autoComplete="off" aria-label="Search" className="site-search-form__input" invalid={invalid} />`
  (Note: This requires the parent SearchInput component to accept and pass an invalid prop.)

issuesFound.title: Form field missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput in SearchInput uses aria-label="Search" as its accessible name, but this is insufficient context for a search input. The label should describe the purpose more clearly, such as "Search Luminary Studio".
