issuesFound.title: Required-field marker announces bare asterisk to AT — add aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The required-field marker in FormGroup renders a visible asterisk (*) without aria-hidden="true". Screen readers announce this as "asterisk", which is not meaningful without additional context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1405: ` *`

issuesFound.title: Required field marked visually only — add aria-required or required attr
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component renders a required-field marker but does not pass the required attribute or aria-required to the input. This means screen readers cannot programmatically determine that the field is required.

issuesFound.title: Form field error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component in FormGroup is rendered but not linked to the input via aria-describedby. This prevents screen readers from announcing the error message in context with the input.

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component in FormGroup does not receive an aria-busy prop when in a loading state. This prevents screen readers from announcing that the button is busy or processing.
issuesFound.fix:

  ```
  <button aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} ...>
  ```

issuesFound.title: Spinner icon missing aria-hidden

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus-visible` selector sets `outline: none` without defining a visible alternative such as `box-shadow`, `border`, or `outline-color` with width. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all interactive elements inheriting this rule, failing WCAG 2.4.7.

issuesFound.title: Form input focus indicator removed without replacement

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static` and `width: auto`, which keeps content in the visual flow instead of hiding it. This clutters the interface for sighted users while intending to hide content from them.

issuesFound.title: Global prefers-reduced-motion media query is empty

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as a box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all interactive elements that do not have a specific override, blocking keyboard-only users from knowing which element is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without defining a visible replacement like box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating to buttons will not see any focus indication, violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on form inputs without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input:focus-visible selector (and select/textarea) sets outline: none without a visible replacement. While :focus has a box-shadow, :focus-visible is the preferred modern standard for keyboard focus and must have its own visible indicator.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually. Screen-reader-only content must be positioned absolutely and clipped to be invisible to sighted users while remaining available to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses transition: transform which causes motion. The corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 533 is empty, failing to disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. This can significantly degrade readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision, violating best practices for text spacing.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector sets color: #aaaaaa on a white background (#ffffff). This ratio is approximately 2.3:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text, making it difficult for users with low vision to read.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm selector sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 Level AA minimum target size of 24px. Users with motor impairments may find it difficult to activate these buttons on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector inherits text-decoration: none and only changes color on hover. Relying solely on color to distinguish links from surrounding text fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color for users who cannot perceive color differences.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. Keyboard users relying on focus indicators will not see which element is focused, making navigation impossible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline without defining a visible focus style. Users navigating via keyboard cannot identify focused buttons, violating WCAG 2.4.7.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on navigation toggle without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see focus on the mobile menu button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on submenu links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot track focus within dropdown menus.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class makes content visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually. Screen-reader-only text will be visible on screen, cluttering the interface.

issuesFound.title: Skip link hidden even when focused
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link is positioned off-screen with transform: translateY(-200%) and the :focus state does not reset this transform. Keyboard users cannot access the skip link as it remains invisible on focus.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link transition on transform is not disabled in the prefers-reduced-motion media query. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the slide animation.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which condenses words and reduces readability for users with cognitive or visual impairments.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing: -0.05em, which condenses letters and reduces readability for users with low vision or dyslexia.

issuesFound.title: Root font size below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, making the base 1rem equal to 12px. Body text using 1rem will be 12px, which is below the recommended 16px for readability.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon selector sets min-width and min-height to 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle selector sets min-height and min-width to 28px. While above 24px, it is below the 44px recommended size without compensating padding.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The footer nav links have text-decoration: none and only change color on hover. Users with color vision deficiencies may not distinguish links from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error state not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds an aria-describedby reference to the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field is in an error state. Without this attribute, users may not be notified that their input is invalid even if the visual error message is present.

issuesFound.title: Form valid state does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the aria-describedby reference when a field becomes valid but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. If a field was previously invalid, the aria-invalid="true" attribute persists, causing screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid even after the user has corrected the error.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module toggles the visibility of the navigation drawer using CSS classes in open() and close() functions but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the _trigger button. Additionally, init() does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" state. Screen reader users will not know whether the menu is currently open or closed.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module manages open/close state via _openInstance and _closeInstance but fails to toggle aria-expanded on the trigger button. The register function also fails to initialize aria-expanded="false". This prevents screen reader users from knowing the state of the dropdown menu.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function sets the internal item.open state to true but does not update the trigger's aria-expanded attribute to "true". The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state where the attribute is never set to true, misleading screen reader users about the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState function in the pricing module updates the visual active class on billing period buttons but fails to synchronize the aria-pressed attribute. Users relying on assistive technology will not be informed which billing period (monthly/annual) is currently selected.

issuesFound.title: Pricing savings message updates without live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateSavingsMessage function dynamically updates the text content of _savingsMsg to reflect discount information, but the element is not initialized with an aria-live attribute in pricing.init. Screen reader users will not be announced the change in savings information when the billing period is toggled.

issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge function updates the text content and aria-label of the product count badge when filters are applied, but it does not announce this change to a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the number of visible products has changed after filtering.

issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector removes the default browser focus outline with outline: none but fails to provide a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border-color change. This causes all interactive elements relying on default focus styles to become invisible to keyboard users, violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 357: :focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline without adding a visible alternative focus indicator such as box-shadow or border-color change. Combined with the global outline removal, this makes button focus states invisible to sighted keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 547: .btn:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Header logo link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible selector removes the focus outline without providing a visible replacement indicator, making the logo link's focus state invisible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 476: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector removes the focus outline without adding a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border-color change, making the mobile menu toggle button's focus state invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 497: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Submenu link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector removes the focus outline without providing a visible alternative indicator, making dropdown menu link focus states invisible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 537: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Account navigation link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector removes the focus outline without adding a visible replacement indicator, making account link focus states invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 445: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Search submit button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector removes the focus outline without providing a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border-color change, making the search button's focus state invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 441: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Feature card link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector removes the focus outline without adding a visible alternative indicator, making card link focus states invisible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 833: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: FAQ button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector removes the focus outline without providing a visible replacement indicator, making accordion button focus states invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1087: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: -3px; box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Footer brand link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand a:focus-visible selector removes the focus outline without adding a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border-color change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1174: .footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible selector removes the focus outline without providing a visible alternative indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1186: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Social link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:focus-visible selector removes the focus outline without adding a visible replacement indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1196: .social-links a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (AA). This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 615: .btn-sm { min-height: 28px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Icon-only buttons are particularly critical for touch accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 627: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class visually broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class incorrectly uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which renders the content visible to sighted users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies. This defeats the purpose of the utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 384: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Danger button insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-danger class uses white text (#ffffff) on a light red background (#ee9999), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.1:1. This fails WCAG AA requirements of 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text, making the button difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 637: .btn-danger { background-color: var(--colour-error-dark); border-color: var(--colour-error-dark); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, violating WCAG 1.4.8 Visual Presentation (AAA) and best practices for readability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 148: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing a non-colour visual differentiator such as border-bottom or font-weight change. This causes links to rely solely on colour to distinguish them from surrounding text, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 163: a { text-decoration: underline; } or add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; to link hover states

issuesFound.title: Button motion animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transition properties for transform and other spatial movements, but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override is provided to disable these animations for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 556: (add after existing .btn rule) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Hero image motion animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector uses transform with perspective and rotate properties and transition with a 500ms duration, but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the 3D rotation animation for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 797: (add after existing transition) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Feature card motion animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card selector includes transition properties for transform (translateY) and other spatial movements, but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the hover lift animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 701: (add after existing .feature-card rule) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card { transition: none; } .feature-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Product card motion animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-card selector includes transition and transform properties for a hover lift effect, but no prefers-reduced-motion override is provided to disable the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 914: (add after existing .product-card rule) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: none; } .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Pricing card motion animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector uses transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) for a hover effect, but the base .pricing-card transition at line 908 lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this spatial animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 908: (add after existing .pricing-card rule) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card { transition: none; } .pricing-card:hover, .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Stat item motion animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-item selector includes transition properties for transform (translateY) on hover, but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the lift animation for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 953: (add after existing .stat-item rule) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item { transition: none; } .stat-item:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Navigation menu items touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a and #nav-menu > li > button selectors set min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. While above the 24px minimum, the small size may still cause activation difficulties without sufficient padding compensation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 507: min-height: 44px; or add padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-4);

issuesFound.title: Search input touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input selector sets height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. Combined with padding of 8px (var(--space-2)), the total height is 44px, but explicit small heights should be avoided.
issuesFound.fix: Line 405: min-height: 44px; height: auto;

issuesFound.title: Filter tabs button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button selector sets min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size and may cause difficulty for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 881: min-height: 44px; or add padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-5);

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class

issuesFound.title: Button transform animation missing reduced motion support
issuesFound.explanation: The button includes transform in its transition property and has an active state with translateY(1px), but lacks a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform animation missing reduced motion support
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has transform transitions and hover effects that create motion, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for this selector is empty, failing to disable the animation for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none !important; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none !important; box-shadow: var(--shadow-2xl); } }

issuesFound.title: Featured pricing card transform animation missing reduced motion support
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card has a transform animation on hover (translateY(-4px) scale(1.01)) that is not covered by the existing reduced motion media query, which only targets the base .pricing-card:hover selector.
issuesFound.fix: Line: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Skip link transform animation missing reduced motion support
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link has a transform transition that moves the element when it appears, but lacks a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em, which reduces the spacing between words and characters. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Step number insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The step number uses white text (var(--colour-text-on-dark) / #ffffff) on a gradient background ending in light purple (#bb88ee). The contrast ratio is approximately 1.6:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line: .step-number { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 56px; height: 56px; flex-shrink: 0; background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--colour-brand-primary), var(--colour-brand-secondary)); color: var(--colour-neutral-900); font-size: var(--font-size-xl); font-weight: var(--font-weight-extrabold); border-radius: var(--radius-full); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md); line-height: 1; user-select: none; letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Newsletter section insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter section uses white text (var(--colour-text-on-dark)) on a gradient background from blue (#5588bb) to light purple (#bb88ee). The contrast ratio against the light purple portion is approximately 1.6:1, failing WCAG AA requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line: .newsletter-section { padding-block: var(--section-gap); background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--colour-brand-primary) 0%, var(--colour-brand-secondary) 100%); color: var(--colour-neutral-900); }

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global anchor selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Without additional non-colour visual differentiators (such as border-bottom or font-weight changes) on the default state, links are only distinguishable by colour, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line: a { text-decoration: underline; } or alternatively add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; to link selectors alongside the text-decoration: none; }

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes focus indicators for all interactive elements relying on this global rule, making keyboard navigation invisible to users.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. Users navigating by keyboard cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Navigation link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which navigation link currently has focus.

issuesFound.title: Submenu link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. Dropdown menu items lack visible focus indicators for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Search button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector removes the outline without replacement. The search submit button lacks a visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. Account navigation links lack focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Feature card link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without replacement. "Learn more" links within feature cards have no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: FAQ button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. Accordion trigger buttons lack visible focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Footer brand link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without replacement. Footer brand links lack visible focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without replacement. Footer navigation links lack visible focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Social link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without replacement. Social media links lack visible focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Table wrapper focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .table-wrapper:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. The scrollable table region lacks a visible focus indicator when focused for keyboard scrolling.

issuesFound.title: Header logo link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible selector removes the outline without replacement. The logo link lacks a visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector removes the outline without replacement. The mobile menu toggle button lacks a visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm selector sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these buttons.

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon selector sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons are particularly difficult for users with tremors or limited dexterity to activate.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size. Checkboxes are difficult to tap accurately on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Search button touch target may be below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets width: 28px and height: 28px without sufficient padding compensation (padding: 0), falling below the 44px recommended touch target size. While above the 24px minimum, it may still be difficult for some users to activate accurately.

issuesFound.title: Screen reader only utility class visually broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only selector uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually. Content intended only for screen readers will be visible to all users, disrupting the visual layout while the comment incorrectly claims it hides content.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em. Negative word-spacing reduces readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities by causing words to visually merge together.

issuesFound.title: Button transition missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property but lacks a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the transform animation on hover/focus.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transition missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector has transition: transform var(--transition-slower) without a reduced motion override. The 3D transform animation on hover can cause vestibular issues for sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Feature card transition missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card selector includes transform in its transition and uses transform: translateY(-2px) on hover without a reduced motion override to disable these spatial animations.

issuesFound.title: Feature card link arrow animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a::after selector transitions transform for the arrow indicator without a reduced motion override to disable this animation.

issuesFound.title: Stat item transition missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-item selector includes transform in its transition and uses transform: translateY(-2px) on hover without a reduced motion override.

issuesFound.title: Pricing card transition missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card selector includes transform in its transition and uses transform: translateY(-2px) on hover without a reduced motion override.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation links rely on color alone
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a selector sets text-decoration: none and only changes color on hover without providing a non-color visual differentiator such as underline, border-bottom, or background change. Users with color vision deficiencies cannot distinguish these links from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links rely on color alone
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector removes text-decoration and only changes color on hover, relying solely on color to distinguish links from surrounding text. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on step number
issuesFound.explanation: The .step-number selector uses white text (var(--colour-text-on-dark)) on a gradient background including #bb88ee (light purple). The contrast ratio is approximately 1.6:1, well below the required 4.5:1 for normal text. Users with low vision cannot read the step numbers.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on recommended plan badge
issuesFound.explanation: The .plan-recommended-badge selector uses white text on var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb). The contrast ratio is approximately 1.9:1, below the required 4.5:1. The "Most popular" badge text is difficult to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on stats labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label selector uses rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) on a gradient background. The resulting contrast ratio is well below 4.5:1, making the statistic descriptions illegible for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on footer bottom text
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-bottom > p selector uses var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) on var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555). The contrast ratio is approximately 1.3:1, below the required 4.5:1. Copyright text is difficult to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector uses var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on #555555 background. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.8:1, below 4.5:1. Footer links are difficult to distinguish and read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on footer address
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand address selector uses var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) on #555555 background, resulting in approximately 1.3:1 contrast. Contact information in the footer is difficult to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on logo bar label
issuesFound.explanation: The .logo-bar-label selector uses var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on var(--colour-neutral-50) (#f8fafc). The contrast ratio is approximately 1.5:1, below 4.5:1. The "Trusted by" label is difficult to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on hero lead text
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-lead selector uses var(--colour-neutral-600) (#475569) on white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.8:1, below the required 4.5:1 for normal text of this size.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on section header paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.9:1, below 4.5:1. Section descriptions are difficult to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on feature card paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card p selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background (var(--colour-bg-page)). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.9:1, below 4.5:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on step content paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: The .step-content p selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.9:1, below 4.5:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on product meta text
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-meta li selector uses var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) on white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.1:1, below 4.5:1. Product metadata is difficult to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on price period text
issuesFound.explanation: The .price-period selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.9:1, below 4.5:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on testimonial captions
issuesFound.explanation: The .testimonial-card figcaption p selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.9:1, below 4.5:1. Author titles in testimonials are difficult to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on input hints
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.9:1, below 4.5:1. Helper text for form inputs is difficult to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on required note
issuesFound.explanation: The .required-note selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.9:1, below 4.5:1. Form instructions are difficult to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on blockquote text
issuesFound.explanation: The blockquote selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.9:1, below 4.5:1. Quoted text is difficult to read.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer open function toggles CSS classes to show the menu but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1830: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The close function hides the mobile navigation drawer but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "false". Screen reader users will be unaware that the menu has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation initialization does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button when the page loads, violating the requirement that ARIA states reflect the initial closed state of the navigation drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1820: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_initialised = true;` in the init function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown open function reveals the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving screen reader users unaware that the dropdown is open and that they can navigate within it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1950: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown close function hides the panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, causing screen readers to incorrectly report the dropdown as still expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1960: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown registration function does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button when the dropdown is first registered, leaving the button without the required state attribute for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1970: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function expands the answer panel but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the question button to "true", preventing screen reader users from knowing which questions are expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2850: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion close function collapses the answer panel but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the question button to "false", leaving screen reader users unaware that the content is now hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2870: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `removeClass(item.el, CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox open function displays the suggestion list but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the search input, so screen reader users are not informed that suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2300: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox close function hides the suggestion list but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the search input, causing screen readers to continue announcing the list as expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2310: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch.init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox initialization does not set aria-expanded="false" on the input element when the page loads, violating the requirement that the initial collapsed state be communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250: Add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');` in the mount function.

issuesFound.title: pricing.init missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module initialization does not set aria-pressed="true" on the initially active billing period toggle button, leaving screen reader users unaware of the default selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2650: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the `_syncToggleState` function or call `_syncToggleState` after ensuring it sets aria-pressed.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates missing aria-pressed update
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as a single-select toggle group but do not update aria-pressed to indicate which tab is currently active, leaving screen reader users unaware of the current filter selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2450: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` immediately after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter.init missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The product filter initialization does not set aria-pressed on the initially active filter tab button, failing to communicate the default active state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2480: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the `_syncTabAriaStates` function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The form validation utility links error messages to fields via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields, so screen reader users are not informed that the field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1100: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clear
issuesFound.explanation: The form validation utility removes error associations but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute to "false" or remove it, causing screen readers to continue reporting the field as invalid after it has been corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1110: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` immediately after the describedby cleanup logic.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._updateCountBadge missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates the product count badge text when filters are applied but does not announce the change via a live region, so screen reader users are not informed how many results match their current filter selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2430: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`);` immediately after `_countBadge.textContent = String(count);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the mobile navigation menu but does not announce the state change via the live region, leaving screen reader users unaware that the menu has opened and focus has moved to the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1830: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);` immediately after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the mobile navigation menu but does not announce the state change, so screen reader users may not realize they have returned to the main page content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);` immediately after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens a dropdown menu but does not announce the expanded state via a live region, which can disorient screen reader users who may not realize additional navigation options are now available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1950: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu opened');` immediately after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes a dropdown menu but does not announce the collapsed state, potentially leaving screen reader users unaware that the menu options are no longer available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1960: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed');` immediately after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the mobile navigation drawer but fails to activate the focus trap, allowing keyboard focus to escape to background elements behind the modal drawer, violating WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1830: Add `if (_trap) _trap.activate();` immediately after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });` and ensure `_trap` is initialized using `createFocusTrap(_drawer)` in the init function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer toggle button does not programmatically update its aria-expanded attribute when the drawer opens or closes. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded/collapsed state of the navigation menu, leaving blind users unaware whether the menu is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels but fails to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Assistive technologies cannot convey the visibility state of the dropdown to users.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed via the open() and close() methods, the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button is not updated to reflect the current state. This breaks the accordion pattern for screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestions combobox does not toggle aria-expanded on the input element when the suggestion listbox opens or closes. Screen readers cannot inform users when the listbox is visible or hidden.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid updates
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validation controller updates visual error states and aria-describedby associations, but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or clear it to "false" on valid fields. Screen readers cannot convey field validity to users.

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the results count updates visually but is not announced to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology are not informed that filtering completed or how many results are showing.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on mobileNav trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have aria-expanded="false" set when the page loads. Screen readers may assume the menu is expanded by default or fail to recognize it as a toggle button.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on siteSearch input
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input lacks an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute. Screen readers cannot correctly interpret the initial collapsed state of the suggestion dropdown.

issuesFound.title: scrollTopBtn missing accessible name verification
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is selected from the DOM but the code does not ensure it has an accessible name (text content or aria-label). If the HTML element lacks text, screen readers will announce it as an unlabeled button.

fpTitles: aria-expanded not initialised on mobileNav trigger

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open function toggles the mobile navigation drawer visibility but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the expanded state. Screen readers will not announce whether the menu is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The close function hides the mobile navigation drawer but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "false". This leaves screen readers with stale state information.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The initialization function sets up the mobile navigation trigger button but fails to set the initial aria-expanded="false" state. Since the menu is closed by default, the trigger must communicate this initial state to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown menu instance, the function reveals the panel but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state of the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When closing a dropdown menu instance, the function hides the panel but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving assistive technologies with incorrect state information.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a new dropdown trigger, the function ensures aria-haspopup and aria-controls but fails to initialize aria-expanded="false" for the closed state. The trigger must communicate its initial collapsed state on page load.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons indicating the active filter state, but the function only updates CSS classes without setting aria-pressed. Toggle buttons must communicate their pressed state to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds the error message to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to convey validation errors.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid cleanup
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation errors from a field, the function removes the error reference from aria-describedby but fails to clear the aria-invalid attribute. The field remains marked as invalid to screen readers even after correction.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._updateCountBadge missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates the product count badge text when filters are applied, but this dynamic content change is not announced to screen readers. Users filtering products receive no feedback on how many results match their criteria.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When switching billing periods (monthly/annual), the function updates price displays dynamically but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users may not realize prices have changed or what the new amounts are.

fpTitles: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not update aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button uses aria-controls and aria-haspopup but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state to screen reader users. This prevents users from knowing whether the submenu is currently visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1130: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ disclosure button controls a panel with aria-controls but does not have aria-expanded to indicate whether the answer panel is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1857: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The close button for the mobile navigation uses IconButton but does not provide the required aria-label prop, leaving screen reader users without an accessible name for the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1210: Add aria-label="Close navigation menu" to the IconButton props.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button uses IconButton without an aria-label, so screen reader users hear only the emoji character without context for what the button does.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1270: Add aria-label={LABELS[theme]} to the IconButton props.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel previous slide button uses IconButton without an aria-label, providing no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1789: Add aria-label="Previous testimonial" to the IconButton props.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel next slide button uses IconButton without an aria-label, providing no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1796: Add aria-label="Next testimonial" to the IconButton props.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to style the field but does not map it to aria-invalid, so screen reader users are not informed when the field has invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 913: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} to the input element.

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not expose it as aria-invalid to assistive technologies, preventing screen reader users from detecting validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 926: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} to the textarea element.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references 'newsletter-email-error' in aria-describedby when in error state, but the error message element has id 'newsletter-status', causing screen readers to fail to announce the error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1933: Change 'newsletter-email-error' to 'newsletter-status'.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The name input references 'contact-name-error' in aria-describedby when in error state, but no element with that id exists in the document, so error messages are not announced to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1990: Remove aria-describedby attribute or ensure FormGroup renders ErrorMessage with id="contact-name-error".

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references 'contact-email-error' in aria-describedby when in error state, but no element with that id exists, preventing screen readers from associating the error message with the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2005: Remove aria-describedby attribute or ensure FormGroup renders ErrorMessage with id="contact-email-error".

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The subject input references 'contact-subject-error' in aria-describedby when in error state, but no element with that id exists, so screen reader users cannot perceive the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2020: Remove aria-describedby attribute or ensure FormGroup renders ErrorMessage with id="contact-subject-error".

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea references 'contact-message-error' in aria-describedby when in error state, but no element with that id exists, preventing error announcement to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2039: Remove 'contact-message-error' from the aria-describedby array or ensure FormGroup renders ErrorMessage with id="contact-message-error".

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons visually indicate the active state but do not use aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to screen reader users, making it impossible to know which filter is active.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page navigation link is visually styled with 'nav-link--current' class but lacks aria-current="page", preventing screen readers from announcing which page is currently active.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active link missing aria-current

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div has onClick without keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop div uses onClick to close the navigation but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers, making it inaccessible to keyboard-only users who cannot click the backdrop.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1036: Add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown={(e) => e.key === 'Enter' && onClose()} to the div, or remove onClick and rely on the Escape key handler.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div has onClick without keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div handles onClick to close the modal but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard support, making the click-to-close functionality inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2193: Add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown={(e) => e.key === 'Enter' && onClose()} to the div, or remove onClick and rely on the Escape key handler.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm focus loss on successful submission
issuesFound.explanation: When the form submits successfully, the form is replaced with a success message. The previously focused submit button is removed from the DOM without moving focus to the success message, causing focus to be lost to the document root.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1916: Add tabIndex={-1} to the success p element and use a ref to focus it when status becomes 'success'.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm focus loss on successful submission
issuesFound.explanation: Upon successful submission, the form is replaced with a success message, removing the submit button from the DOM. Focus is not moved to the success message, causing keyboard focus to be lost.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1970: Add tabIndex={-1} to the success p element and use a ref to focus it when status becomes 'success'.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility does not hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class is intended to hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers, but the implementation uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible — the opposite of what is required. Content marked with .sr-only remains fully visible to all users, defeating the purpose of the utility and potentially causing layout issues.
issuesFound.fix: Line 287-295: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule removes the default outline without providing any visible alternative focus indicator such as box-shadow or border. This leaves keyboard users without any visual feedback when navigating interactive elements, violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible (AA).
issuesFound.fix: Line 267-270: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Within the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible rule removes the outline without providing any visible alternative. In high-contrast mode, this leaves keyboard users with no focus indicator at all, blocking navigation entirely for users who rely on this accessibility feature.
issuesFound.fix: Line 279-281: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stat label
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label uses rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (white at 30% opacity) on a gradient background of #5588bb to #bb88ee. The resulting contrast ratio is approximately 1.6:1, far below the required 4.5:1 for normal text. Users with low vision cannot read this text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 861: .stat-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85); line-height: var(--line-height-snug); max-width: 160px; text-align: center; margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm selector sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 (AA) minimum of 24px. Users with motor impairments or touchscreens will have difficulty activating this button reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 381: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon selector sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, both below the WCAG 2.5.8 (AA) minimum of 24px. Icon-only buttons are particularly difficult to target accurately on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 392-396: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] sets width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, all below the WCAG 2.5.8 (AA) minimum of 24px. Checkboxes are critical form controls that require adequate touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1090: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words by 20%. Negative word-spacing makes text harder to read, particularly for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and can cause words to appear merged together.
issuesFound.fix: Line 168: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The rule a { text-decoration: none; } removes underlines from all links globally. Without underlines, links in body text are distinguished by colour alone, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Colour. Users with colour vision deficiencies cannot distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 194: a { text-decoration: underline; } and add Line 196: .prose a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Base font-size below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The html selector sets font-size: 12px, which is below the recommended 16px (1rem) baseline. This affects all text on the page, making it harder to read for users with low vision or presbyopia, and forcing users to zoom or increase text size manually.
issuesFound.fix: Line 159: html { scroll-behavior: smooth; text-size-adjust: 100%; -webkit-text-size-adjust: 100%; font-size: 16px; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); tab-size: 4; -moz-tab-size: 4; height: 100%; }

issuesFound.title: Line-height below 1.5 on paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: The p selector sets line-height: 1, which is extremely tight and well below the recommended 1.5 minimum for body text. Tight line-height impairs readability for users with dyslexia, cognitive disabilities, or low vision by making it difficult to track from line to line.
issuesFound.fix: Line 183: p { line-height: 1.5; text-wrap: pretty; }

issuesFound.title: Empty reduced-motion media query
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty and does not override any animations or transitions. Users who have requested reduced motion will still experience all animations, which can cause vestibular disorders, motion sensitivity, or distraction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 297-298: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property and uses transform: translateY(1px) on :active. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule that disables this transform animation, so users requesting reduced motion still experience motion effects.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 339: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transform transition and perspective/rotate transforms on hover. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 613-618 contains empty rules that do not disable the transforms, leaving motion-sensitive users exposed to the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 613-618: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs remove outline on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see which checkbox is focused when navigating forms.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1191-1192: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on footer brand links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove outline on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users navigating the footer cannot see focused links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1231-1233: .footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on footer navigation links without replacement

issuesFound.fix: Line 1236-1238: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on social links without replacement
