issuesFound.fix: Line 1244-1246: .social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24px. Users with motor impairments or using touch screens may have difficulty activating these buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 456-458: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon-only buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24px. Icon-only buttons are particularly difficult to activate accurately on touch screens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 462-466: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has width: 28px and height: 28px, which is below the recommended 44×44px touch target. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the search button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 576-577: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs are set to width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24px. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty checking boxes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1201-1202: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Transform animation without reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition, causing spatial movement on hover/active states. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion, which may cause vestibular discomfort.
issuesFound.fix: Line 447-452: .btn { ... transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast); ... } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Transform animation with empty reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transform transition, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 695-699 is empty and does not disable the animation. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the 3D transform effect on hover.
issuesFound.fix: Line 695-699: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; box-shadow: var(--shadow-2xl); } }

issuesFound.title: Transform animation without reduced-motion override on skip link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link has a transform transition for revealing itself on focus, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort from the sliding animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 332: .skip-link { ... transform: translateY(-200%); transition: transform var(--transition-base); ... } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. Negative word-spacing can impair readability for users with dyslexia, cognitive disabilities, or low vision by making it harder to distinguish word boundaries.
issuesFound.fix: Line 79: body { ... word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on hero lead paragraph
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-lead paragraph uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.24:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements of 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 645-649: .hero-lead { font-size: var(--font-size-xl); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); margin: 0; max-width: 480px; text-wrap: pretty; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on section header descriptions
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background. The contrast ratio of approximately 2.24:1 fails WCAG AA. At 18px font-size, large text requires 3:1 minimum.
issuesFound.fix: Line 751-755: .section-header p { font-size: var(--font-size-lg); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on how-it-works description
issuesFound.explanation: The .how-it-works > p uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on --colour-bg-subtle (#f8fafc). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.3:1, failing WCAG AA requirements for 18px text (requires 3:1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 804-809: .how-it-works > p { text-align: center; font-size: var(--font-size-lg); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); max-width: var(--container-sm); margin-inline: auto; margin-bottom: var(--space-14); padding-inline: var(--space-6); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on product descriptions
issuesFound.explanation: Product card descriptions use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white background. The contrast ratio of approximately 2.24:1 fails WCAG AA for 14px text (requires 4.5:1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 872-877: .product-body > p:not(.product-tagline) { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; flex-grow: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on plan taglines
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing card taglines use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white background. The contrast ratio of approximately 2.24:1 fails WCAG AA for 14px text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 969-973: .plan-tagline { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); margin: 0; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on form hints
issuesFound.explanation: Form input hints use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white background. The contrast ratio of approximately 2.24:1 fails WCAG AA for 12px text (requires 4.5:1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1195: .input-hint { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--colour-neutral-500); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without non-colour visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a { text-decoration: none; } rule removes underlines from all links. Links in body text are distinguished from surrounding text only by colour, violating WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color). Users with colour vision deficiencies may not be able to distinguish links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 103: a { text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; } a:hover { text-decoration-thickness: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Base font-size below 16px impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, which becomes the base for all rem units. This results in body text of 12px (1rem), below the recommended 16px minimum for comfortable reading. Users with low vision or reading difficulties may struggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 73: html { scroll-behavior: smooth; text-size-adjust: 100%; -webkit-text-size-adjust: 100%; font-size: 16px; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); tab-size: 4; -moz-tab-size: 4; height: 100%; }

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line-height below minimum impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The p element has line-height: 1, which is below the recommended minimum of 1.5 for body text. Single line-height makes paragraphs difficult to read, especially for users with dyslexia, cognitive disabilities, or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 139: p { line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); text-wrap: pretty; }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger lacks initial aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button when the page loads. The attribute is only set after user interaction, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2758: After `_initialised = true;`, add: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers lack initial aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets aria-haspopup but does not set aria-expanded="false" on trigger buttons during initialization.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2936: After setting aria-haspopup, add: `if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState() function toggles CSS active classes on billing period buttons but never sets aria-pressed. Toggle buttons must communicate their state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3552: Replace the toggleClass loop with: `_periodBtns.forEach(btn => { const isActive = btn.getAttribute('data-period') === period; toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); });`

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function only toggles CSS active classes on filter tabs. Filter buttons acting as toggle controls must use aria-pressed to communicate state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3247: Replace with: `qsa('[data-filter-value]', _tabsEl).forEach(btn => { const isActive = btn.getAttribute('data-filter-value') === _productFilterState.activeCategory; toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); });`

issuesFound.title: Product filter changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function updates visible product cards and count badges, but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users cannot perceive that content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3227: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied('category') || `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod() function updates price displays across all plan cards but does not announce the billing period change to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3528: After `_updateSavingsMessage(period);`, add: `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? (STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Switched to annual billing') : (STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || 'Switched to monthly billing'));`

issuesFound.title: Search combobox input lacks aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.mount() function sets role="combobox" and aria-haspopup="listbox" on the search input, but never sets aria-expanded. Combobox pattern requires aria-expanded to communicate listbox state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3846: Add: `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` and update in suggestionListRenderer.show(): `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in hide(): `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: SUPPORTS.dom reference is undefined causing potential runtime error
issuesFound.explanation: The createFocusTrap function references `SUPPORTS.dom` which does not exist in the SUPPORTS object defined earlier. This will cause a ReferenceError when the focus trap tries to add event listeners.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2362: Replace `if (SUPPORTS.dom) {` with `if (typeof document !== 'undefined') {` and Line 2370: Replace the same condition.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner lacks proper role and focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The dynamically created cookie banner sets role="dialog" and aria-modal="true", but does not set aria-labelledby to reference the title element, and the title element's id may not be unique if multiple banners are created.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5260: Add: `'id': 'cookie-banner-title-' + shortId(),` to the title element, and Line 5257: Add: `'aria-labelledby': 'cookie-banner-title-' + shortId(),` to the banner element (using the same generated id).

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard progress bar lacks proper aria-valuetext
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateProgress() function sets aria-valuenow and aria-valuetext, but aria-valuetext uses a template that may not be defined in STRINGS, potentially resulting in undefined text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4522: Replace with: `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuetext', (STRINGS.wizardProgressText || 'Step {n} of {total}').replace('{n}', String(stepIndex + 1)).replace('{total}', String(_steps.length)));`

fpTitles: Cookie consent banner lacks proper role and focus management
Multi-step wizard progress bar lacks proper aria-valuetext

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function links an error message to a field via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot announce that the field contains invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1380-1390: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` in the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error message reference but never clears aria-invalid from the field. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid even after the error is corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1390-1410: Add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` at the end of the markValid function after the aria-describedby cleanup.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions toggle panel visibility via hidden attribute and CSS classes but never set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users have no way to know the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3060: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside _openInstance after `inst.open = true;`
  Line 3075: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside _closeInstance after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle switch element (_toggleEl) is assigned role="switch" when not a native button, but aria-checked is never set or updated. A switch role requires aria-checked to communicate its state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4268: inside _syncToggleState, after `const isYear = period === 'year';`, add `if (_toggleEl) setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));`

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters does not announce filter results to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the visible card count changes but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that the product list has been filtered or how many results remain.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3660: inside the filterAnimation.run callback, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(totalMatches) || `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod does not announce billing period change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the prices update visually but no live region announcement informs screen reader users of the change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4285: after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? (STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Switched to annual billing') : (STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || 'Switched to monthly billing'));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function sets up event listeners but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. The initial state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2955: inside init(), after `_initialised = true;`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not initialize aria-expanded on registered triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but never sets aria-expanded="false" on trigger buttons. The initial collapsed state is not exposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3145: inside register(), after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input field collects personal data (email address) but lacks the autocomplete attribute. This prevents browsers from correctly autofilling the user's email address, impacting users with cognitive or motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5620: inside newsletterForm.init(), after `_emailEl = qs('input[type="email"]', _formEl);`, add `if (_emailEl && !_emailEl.getAttribute('autocomplete')) _emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Contact form inputs missing autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form collects personal data (full name, email) but the inputs lack autocomplete attributes. This prevents browsers from correctly offering autofill suggestions for name and email fields.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5780: inside contactForm.init(), after binding each field, add autocomplete attributes:
  Line 5785: `const nameField = qs('[name="full_name"]', _formEl); if (nameField && !nameField.getAttribute('autocomplete')) nameField.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');`
  Line 5786: `const emailField = qs('[name="email"]', _formEl); if (emailField && !emailField.getAttribute('autocomplete')) emailField.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

fpTitles: Newsletter email input missing autocomplete attribute
Contact form inputs missing autocomplete attributes

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The NavDropdown trigger button has aria-controls and aria-haspopup but lacks aria-expanded. Screen reader users cannot tell whether the dropdown menu is open or closed, as the button's state is not communicated programmatically.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop for icon-only buttons, but the mobile nav close button passes only an onClick handler and className. Screen readers will announce only the visual character "✕" without context.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 839-842:
```tsx
          <IconButton
            aria-label="Close navigation menu"
            onClick={onClose}
            className="mobile-nav__close"
          >
```

issuesFound.title: Theme toggle button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle IconButton renders only an emoji icon with no aria-label. Screen reader users hear only the emoji character with no indication of the button's purpose or current theme state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 956-961:
```tsx
    <IconButton
      aria-label={LABELS[theme]}
      onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])}
      className="theme-toggle"
    >
```

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons visually indicate active/inactive state through CSS classes but lack aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot identify which filter is currently selected.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ item trigger button has aria-controls pointing to the answer panel but lacks aria-expanded. Screen reader users cannot tell whether each FAQ answer is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: Carousel slides missing aria-hidden when not visible
issuesFound.explanation: Testimonial carousel slides that are not currently visible lack aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may announce all slides simultaneously, creating a confusing experience instead of presenting only the active slide.

issuesFound.title: Carousel navigation buttons missing accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The previous and next buttons in the testimonials carousel are IconButtons that render only arrow characters (‹ and ›) without aria-label props. Screen reader users cannot identify the purpose of these navigation buttons.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1451-1462:
```tsx
          <IconButton
            aria-label="Previous testimonial"
            aria-controls={trackId}
            onClick={handlePrev}
            className="testimonials-track__prev"
          >
            ‹
          </IconButton>
          <IconButton
            aria-label="Next testimonial"
            aria-controls={trackId}
            onClick={handleNext}
            className="testimonials-track__next"
          >
            ›
          </IconButton>
```

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog element. Screen readers may not correctly identify the dialog as modal, potentially allowing focus to escape or failing to announce the dialog properly.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input has broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input's aria-describedby references id "newsletter-email-error" when there is an error, but no element with that id exists because the FormGroup component never receives the errorId prop. Screen readers cannot announce the error message associated with the input.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1613-1636:
```tsx
          <FormGroup
            label="Email address"
            htmlFor="newsletter-email"
            errorId="newsletter-email-error"
            errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
          >
            <TextInput
              id="newsletter-email"
              type="email"
              name="email"
              placeholder="you@company.com"
              autoComplete="email"
              value={email.state.value}
              onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)}
              onBlur={email.onBlur}
              required
              invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error}
              aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined}
            />
          </FormGroup>
```

issuesFound.title: Contact form inputs have broken aria-describedby references
issuesFound.explanation: All contact form inputs reference error message ids in aria-describedby, but the FormGroup components never receive errorId props. The error messages are never rendered with the referenced ids, breaking the association between inputs and their error descriptions.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1720-1790 (showing name field as example, apply same pattern to all fields):
```tsx
          <FormGroup
            label="Full name"
            htmlFor="contact-name"
            required
            errorId="contact-name-error"
            errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}
          >
            <TextInput
              id="contact-name"
              name="name"
              type="text"
              autoComplete="name"
              required
              aria-required="true"
              value={name.state.value}
              onChange={(ev) => name.onChange(ev.target.value)}
              onBlur={name.onBlur}
              invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error}
              aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined}
            />
          </FormGroup>
```
